description: Returns the authenticated user's GitHub identity.
rationale: Read-only. Returns only the calling user's own profile data. No external side effects.

description: Searches GitHub repositories by query string.
rationale: Read-only search. No mutations. Results do not include sensitive data beyond what is publicly available.
Read-only. Retrieves existing issue metadata. No external side effects.

description: Lists commits for a repository branch.
rationale: Read-only. Returns commit history metadata. No external side effects.

description: Retrieves the contents of a file from a GitHub repository.
rationale: Read operation but accesses file contents directly, which may include sensitive configuration, credentials references, or proprietary data. Traverses access boundaries.

description: Retrieves details of a pull request including diff content.
rationale: Read operation but may expose source code changes, review comments, and contributor information. Limited external interaction.

description: Adds a comment to a pending pull request review.
rationale: Additive side effect but non-destructive. Review is still pending and not published. Reversible prior to review submission.

description: Creates a new issue in a GitHub repository.
rationale: Writes to an external system. Notifications are sent to repository subscribers. Not easily undone without closing or deleting the issue.

description: Updates the title, body, or state of an existing pull request.
rationale: Mutates an existing external resource. Changes are visible to collaborators and may affect CI or review workflows.

description: Creates or updates a file in a GitHub repository.
rationale: Writes directly to repository content. Changes are committed and visible in history. Affects production branches if not scoped to a feature branch.

description: Merges a pull request into the target branch.
rationale: Integrates changes into the target branch. High impact if the PR affects a protected branch. Reversible only via revert commit.

description: Deletes a branch from a GitHub repository.
rationale: Irreversible if the branch is not backed up locally. Unmerged commits on the branch may be lost. No confirmation or undo mechanism.
